name: Gir
trait: A2 milk with high butterfat. Hardy, gentle, the classic Indian dairy breed.
fact: Gir cows have a distinctive convex forehead and pendulous ears — designed by climate, not breeding committees.

name: Sahiwal
trait: India's finest dairy breed. Heat-tolerant, calm-tempered, consistently high-yielding.
fact: A Sahiwal cow can produce milk in 40 °C heat that would shut down a Holstein-Friesian's lactation.

name: Tharparkar
trait: Dual-purpose, drought-resistant, deep-chested. The cow that survives where others can't.
fact: Named for the Thar Parkar region. Was nearly lost during Partition; conservation programmes brought the breed back.

name: Bachaur
trait: Compact, strong, traditionally a draught + dairy breed. Quiet temperament.
fact: Named for the Bachaur region near the Nepal border. Less famous than Gir or Sahiwal but every bit as important to local farming heritage.

name: Punganur
trait: One of the smallest cattle breeds in the world. Dwarf stature, A2 milk with very high butterfat. Sacred in temple traditions.
fact: An adult Punganur stands about 70–90 cm at the shoulder. A 5-year-old child looks her in the eye.
